---
title: "The Effect of Runtime and Genre on IMDB Ratings"
author: "Team Name"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(knitr)
library(readr)
library(broom)
library(pandoc)
output_dir <- "../gen/output"
```

## Introduction

*TODO*

## Descriptive Statistics

The table below summarizes the number of movies, average rating, and average runtime for each genre in our dataset.
```{r desc-stats}
descriptive_stats <- read_csv(file.path(output_dir, "descriptive_stats.csv"))
kable(descriptive_stats, caption = "Descriptive Statistics by Genre")
```
```{r movie-count, fig.cap="Movie Count by Genre", out.width="80%", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(output_dir, "movie_count.png"))
```
```{r ratings-genre, fig.cap="Rating Distribution by Genre", out.width="80%", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(output_dir, "ratings_by_genre.png"))
```

## Visual Exploration: Runtime vs. Rating by Genre

The plot below shows the relationship between runtime and average rating separately for each genre.
```{r interaction-plot, fig.cap="Runtime vs. Rating by Genre", out.width="90%", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(output_dir, "interaction_plot.png"))
```

## Model 1: Main Effects

```{r lm-main}
lm_main <- readRDS(file.path(output_dir, "lm_main.rds"))
kable(tidy(lm_main, conf.int = TRUE), digits = 4, caption = "Main Effects Model Coefficients")
kable(glance(lm_main), digits = 4, caption = "Main Effects Model Summary")
```

## Model 2: Interaction Effects

```{r lm-interaction}
lm_interaction <- readRDS(file.path(output_dir, "lm_interaction.rds"))
kable(tidy(lm_interaction, conf.int = TRUE), digits = 4, caption = "Interaction Model Coefficients")
kable(glance(lm_interaction), digits = 4, caption = "Interaction Model Summary")
```

```{r interaction-terms}
interaction_terms <- readRDS(file.path(output_dir, "interaction_terms.rds"))
kable(interaction_terms, digits = 4, caption = "Runtime × Genre Interaction Terms")
```

## Model Comparison

```{r anova}
anova_result <- readRDS(file.path(output_dir, "anova_result.rds"))
kable(tidy(anova_result), digits = 4, caption = "ANOVA: Main Effects vs. Interaction Model")
```
```{r model-comparison}
model_comparison <- read_csv(file.path(output_dir, "model_comparison.csv"))
kable(model_comparison, digits = 4, caption = "Model Fit Comparison")
```

## Conclusion

*TODO*
